records.m_pText: Nothing Brave About an Empty Stomach
*sigh*$nI don’t understand.
Why won’t that Great Chasm close up?$nI just don’t understand.
What was it the dark knight said$nabout me?
…I believe he called you a$n“peculiar fairy.”
Then he said that he would slay you,$nand that “this will end”$nonce you’re gone.
He…he really said all that?
Well, I don’t care if he’s an enemy,$nthat’s just plain rude.$n*sniffle*
Oh, Datz.$nHave you seen [PCM2]?
Last time I saw him,$nhe said he was going to$nthe hold to rest.
Wonder if he’s alright.$nHe looked an awful shade of pale.
I must apologize to you, Datz,$nand you too, Zatz.
Come again, Lady Vestal?
What could you have possibly done$nto require an apology to us?
Though we have now twice ventured$ninto the Holy Pillar, the Great Chasm$nshows no sign of closing.
I have put you two and the$nproprietress in grave peril—$nseemingly to no end.
Oh, so that’s what you’re going$non about. Well, never you mind,$nLady Vestal, because we don’t!
Yeah, we’re here to protect you,$nwherever this adventure may$ntake us!
Couldn’t have said it better myself!$nWe live by our own creed,$nand protecting you is part of that!
Besides, those were Commander$nGoodman’s orders! So don’t you$nworry about nothing, Lady Vestal.
Same goes for the proprietress.
She’d just laugh off your apology$nand make you some food.
…Thank you…all of you.
Everyone! The proprietress wants$nus all to meet in the tavern!
“It’s times like these that you$nneed some good grub in your belly,”$nshe says.
“After all, you can’t Brave on$nan empty stomach!”
Ah ha ha!$nWell, let’s go, [PCF1]!
Yes, let’s.
Bird Watching
Amazing…$nThere’s nothing but old dead trees,$nyet it’s full of bird life.
That bird! I could swear$nI’ve seen it before!
That would be a Luxen sea swallow.
Really? A Luxen sea swallow?$nI know I’ve seen one before,$nbut where?
You must’ve seen one back in$nyour homeland. Most likely in$nthe summer.
Yes, now I remember! But how$ncould you possibly know that?
Ha ha ha, the Luxen sea swallow$nis well known for its migration across$nLuxendarc’s northern hemisphere.
They spend a short summer in Eternia,$nspring and fall in Caldisla…
…then winter in Florem,$nwhere it’s warmer.
That was impressive, [PCM1]!$nYou’re quite an expert on the subject.
Not really. It’s just Luxen sea$nswallows pass through Norende$naround planting time…
…and then again around the harvest.$nThey’re one of the signs$nof the seasons.
Not bad, [PCM1], not bad.$nLet me see if I can give you some$nnon-Norende pointers, though.
For instance, that black falcon$non that branch over there…
That’s a Flor crow. But its$ncommon name is the flower crow.$nIt’s indigenous to this land.
Ah…yes. Well done.$nI was testing you, you see.
What about that Flor goose in$nthat pond over there?$nDid you know—
Sure, it stores the energy it needs for$nits migration as fat in its liver.
That trait is exploited by man in$nthe creation of foie gras.
Ulp!!
[PCM1], you are like a living$nencyclopedia of bird watching!
Try as he might to impress with$nnotes scribbled on his hand,$n[PCM2] cannot match the$ndepth of your knowledge!
Ooh, ouch. Poor [PCM2]…
Don’t worry, [PCM2]!$nI for one appreciate the$neffort you made!
*sigh*
Without a Cherry on Top
Special parfait!$nSpecial parfait!
Eternian special…parfait!!!
It starts with a honey pancake,$ndouble-rolled, thick and fluffy!
…Um, [PCF2], listen—
Then five irresistible scoops of$nice cream on top!
Vanilla, green tea, mint,$nstrawberry cheesecake, and$nchocolate!
[PCF2], sorry to interrupt$nyour food fantasy…
But didn’t you just say your$ndiet was going so well?
You are your own master.$nOnly you have control of yourself.
Weren’t you going on about boosting$nyour femininity by dieting?
Tsk, mind your own business,$nall of you!
I know how to control myself!
I’ll be back on my diet tomorrow!
But they say those who cannot today,$ncannot forever.
That’s right! How can you ever$nsucceed with such weak willpower?
W-weak willpower!? I’ll have you$nknow it’s plenty strong!
I’ll show you. I’m back on my diet$nfrom this moment!
That’s the spirit, [PCF2]!
And I’ll start by forgoing the cherry$nthat goes on top of the parfait,$nterribly difficult though it is!
Airy’s Temper
[PCM2], could I have a$nword with you?
I can’t have you doing that again!
Huh? Oh, you mean asking questions$nbefore the Rite of Awakening?
That’s right.$nI need to focus before I begin.
But how can I when you suddenly$nupset me like that!?
I upset you?
I was merely asking what would$nhappen if the crystal was destroyed.
Or was it when I asked why we can’t$ndo without its blessings?
Or…was it when I said we had$nfailed to rid the world of$ndarkness two times already?$nDid that strike a nerve?
S-strike a nerve!?
What’re you talking about!?$nWhat nerve could that have$npossibly struck?
Tsk! You’re just plain rude!$nAnd annoying to boot!
……$nAlright, alright already.
You don’t have to shout.$nI hear you loud and clear.
You’re not exactly helping$nmy headache.
And truth be told,$n[PCF1] is the one who really$nneeds to concentrate during the$nrite, not you, Airy.
Hmph!$nYou sure have a big mouth!
Hey, I’m not done with you yet!
You get back here,$n[PCM2]!
[PCF2]’s Parfait Binge
Where is [PCM2]?
He’s up on deck with [PCM1].$nSomething about having a$nman-to-man talk.
I see. [PCM2] has been acting$nstrangely of late, has he not?
I suppose so.
Yes! Yes he has!
I wonder if something is wrong.
Perhaps he remembered some$nunpleasant event from his past.
An unpleasant event from his$npast!?
Is that why he’s been$ngiving me a hard time!?$nWell, I won’t stand for it!
If he gets in our way again,$nwe should kick him off the ship!
Stop it, Airy! I don’t see why$nyou’re making such a fuss!
We’re talking about one of our$ncomrades—someone who lost$nhis memory and nearly his$nsanity, too.
Surely you could forgive such a$nminor thing as this!
…[PCF2]!$nWhere are you going?
To see the proprietress!$nI’ll be drowning my sorrow$nin parfaits tonight!$nAnd don’t try to stop me!
……$nI-I can’t believe it!
Did you hear that, [PCF1]!?$n[PCF2] was so mean!
The Monkey Show
Look, over there!
Those monkeys are playing$nflower checkers!
Ah, one of those monkey shows$nthis town is famous for.
Flower…checkers?$nWhat exactly is that?
It’s a game that originated in Florem.$nYou play it on a board using pieces$nrepresenting different flowers.
Along with Caldislan chess,$nit’s one of the most challenging$ngames there is.
But what about the monkeys?$nThey’re not really playing, are they?
They’re probably just moving$nthe pieces around.
You’d think, right?$nWell, I actually played that$nmonkey over there yesterday.
Really!? How did it go?
Ha ha ha, I wiped the floor with him!$nI mean, they really are just$nrandomly moving pieces around!
I thought so.
That is to be expected. It is a$nthinking man’s game, after all.
Yep! And I won three out of five$ngames! It’s like that old saying,$n“Monkey see, monkey do.”
Uh, [PCM2]…
[PCM2]’s Invitation
[PCF2], how about we get$nsome dinner tonight,$njust the two of us?
Just the two of us?
I’ve already made a reservation$nat the Tri-Flower, the restaurant$nat the very top of the Central$nFlor Hotel.
They serve a full-course Florem$ndinner! Isn’t that your favorite,$n[PCF2]?
We can enjoy the night view$nwhile we eat!
I think…I think I’ll pass!
Listen, [PCF2],$nI want you to know something.
I don’t go around asking out$njust anyone like this anymore.
…I know, [PCM2].
And I don’t go around rejecting$njust anyone, either.
Huh? Wait…huh!?$nWhat’s that supposed to—
[PCF2] delivers a bruising$nblow to the male ego!$nThat one’s got to hurt!
Oh, ha ha… Ha ha ha… Ha…$n*sigh*
[PCF2] and the Ghost Ship
L-let’s get out of here, already!
I’ve had enough of this creepy$nghost ship!
Yeah, and there aren’t any$nwomen here, either.
Your womanizing is so far beyond$nappalling that it’s almost impressive.
[PCF1], you’re not scared of$nghosts, are you?
Not at all. Rather, I feel quite$nsorry for them.
Life is a miraculous gift born of$nthe harmony between the four crystals.
And once a soul’s life has come$nto an end, it may be reborn again$nby returning to the crystals.
But ghosts, on the other hand,$nare doomed to haunt this world.$nSuch pitiable creatures.
First of all, our teachings tell$nus that—
I’m not really following all this,$nbut does it go on much longer?
Well, there are several beliefs as to$nwhere the souls of the dead go,$nbut according to Crystalist thought—
I know this is a really important$ntopic, but I’m sorry, I just can’t$ntake any more!
Yes, it is a really important topic!$nNow, as I was saying—
No… Please… For the love of$nthe crystals, stop!
To Each His Own
Let’s take a coffee break,$neveryone.
Here you go, [PCM2]…$nStrong and black.
Thanks.
For you, [PCM1], weak with$nmilk and two lumps of sugar.
Thank you.
And for you, [PCF1], tea.$nStill not used to coffee?
Thank you.$nCoffee makes my head go all fuzzy.
I hope you will forgive me,$n[PCF2], making you go$nthrough the extra trouble$nlike this.
Ah ha ha, it’s not your fault.$nAfter all, you never even tried$ncoffee until you left your temple.
Blech… This isn’t black.$nYou sure this isn’t [PCM1]’s?
Really? Then whose is this?$n…Augh, it’s way too sweet!$nIt must be yours, [PCF2]!
Who puts this much sugar in their$ncoffee, anyway!? It’s a grainy$nslush at the bottom.
Then this must be [PCM2]’s…$nUgh, that’s awfully bitter.
Well, [PCF1], at least you’re$nnot the only one making me go$nthrough extra trouble.
…Ha ha ha.
Two Peas in a Pod
So beautiful…
The passion of a mature love$naffair is something special.
The passion of what?$nWhat’re you going on about?
I don’t know about passion, but$nBarras is as lively and loud as$never, with that booming voice.
And Holly’s the only one who can hold$nher own in a conversation with him.$nThey can talk for days!
It’s wonderful to have$nsomeone like that…
If both are the talkative type,$nbetter they speak of love for each$nother than quarrel and hurl insults.
Oh, my! I didn’t expect to hear$nanything like that from you,$n[PCF1]!
It seems people truly can change!
I did not mean it that way.
That way? And what exactly$nwould “that way” be, hmm?
What I meant was the two of them$nare as two peas in a pod.
They are both frank to a fault and$nput on no airs. And neither are$nthey afraid to bare their souls.
Perhaps happiest of all are$nkindred spirits—alike in most$nevery way—who find each other.
Is that so?$n…Hold on!
Who were you really referring to,$n[PCF1]? Who do you think is$nyour kindred spirit!?$nCome on, out with it!
Oh, umm…I… *ahem*$n……
Some might say living too long$nwith someone like yourself$nmight drive you crazy.
And a couple with two people like me?$nThat would get old rather quick,$ndon’t you think?
Ha ha, would it really be that bad?$nWhat do you think, [PCF1]?
Who, me!?$nErr…well, ha ha.$nI suppose it wouldn’t be all that bad.
What?$nWhy’re you staring at us like that?
Huh?
The True Valor of a Knight
Knight Argent Heinkel…he made a$nlasting impression, alright.$nHe wasn’t even afraid to die.
Well, of course he wasn’t.$nThe ultimate quality of an Eternian$nknight is scorning fear in the$nface of death.
That is part of the duchy’s code of$nchivalry and proof of a knight’s valor.
To fearlessly face death for a$ncause you believe in is a truly$nadmirable personal creed.
But as a doctrine that forces$nothers to prove their valor so?$nI am not sure I agree.
To say, “Do not fear death!” and$nencourage such a mindset…
…I believe it welcomes a cold,$nmindless disregard for life.
Maybe so, but they say knights$nthickly clad in courage and$nhardened against emotion…
…make the most formidable$nforce on any battlefield.
Well, I don’t know about the$nother officers, but Heinkel at$nleast is a different sort.
I still remember the code of$nchivalry he drilled into me$nfrom a young age.$nHe used to always tell me…
“Valor is not fearing death.”
“But when life becomes even more$nterrifying than death…”
“…survival becomes the truest$nvalor there is.”
I see. So his was the truest$ncode of chivalry of all.
Mm-hm!
Spell Blades
That support ability auto-aspir$nblade is something else.
It lets you attack your opponent’s$nMP with a conventional attack,$nand absorb it, too.
Just use Brave, then conventional$nattacks between magic attacks.$nYou’ll never want for MP!
It’s perfect for you, [PCF1]!
Oh, I never thought of that.
Why are you telling us this now,$n[PCM1]?
I just wanted to let you know how$nwonderful spell blades could be.
It can really help you, too,$n[PCF2].
You could add some sort of$nelement to your blade attack.
Start with Brave, then cast a$nspell blade. You can attack$nright away and even switch to$nanother spell blade if you want.
Spell blades are incredible,$naren’t they!?
Not as incredible as your$nendorsement of them.
If they’re so great, why don’t you$nchange your job to spell fencer,$n[PCM1]?
Oh, um…uhh…$nThis wasn’t how the conversation$nwas supposed to go.
And how was it supposed to go?
(Uh-oh, what do I do now?)
Hey, [PCM2], what am I$nsupposed to say in this case?
……$n(…Ack! The cat’s out of the bag!)
You said to get [PCF1] and$n[PCF2] into those flimsy$nspell fencer outfits…
…I should “sell them hard” on$nthe job’s merits…
And well, I said exactly what you$ntold me to, but their reply$nwas different.
So what do I say now?
Yes, tell us, [PCM2]…$nWhat does he say now?
I can’t wait to hear this!
